name: OpenAI o4-mini
archDisplay: Dense transformer reasoning model, compact (exact size undisclosed)
note: Compact reasoning model with strong price/performance and native tool use.

results.notes: MathVista visual math reasoning, o4-mini, high reasoning effort. Read directly from a mirrored copy of OpenAI's official launch blog (o1=71.8, o3=86.8, o4-mini=72.0) and cross-confirmed via independent web search corroboration.
CharXiv-Reasoning (scientific figure reasoning drawn from arXiv papers), o4-mini, high reasoning effort; from OpenAI's official launch blog (o1=55.1, o3=78.6, o4-mini=72.0).

BrowseComp agentic browsing, o4-mini with python+browsing tools, high reasoning effort. From OpenAI's official launch blog chart (4o+browsing=1.9, o3=49.7, o4-mini=28.3, deep research=51.5); confirmed by independent secondary-source search corroboration.
Aider Polyglot code editing, diff format, o4-mini-high (whole-format score is 68.9%). Diff-format figure used to match this corpus's existing aider-polyglot convention (e.g. gpt-5.json).

verificationNotes: Confirmed real. developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/o4-mini fetched directly: verified 200K context, 100K max output, $1.10/$4.40 per 1M input/output pricing, text+image-in modalities — all match exactly. Independent secondary sources (DataCamp, Scale AI HLE leaderboard) corroborate all six benchmark figures (AIME 93.4, GPQA 81.4, SWE-bench 68.1, MMMU 81.6, HLE 17.7, Codeforces 2719) as OpenAI's self-reported launch-day numbers from the o3/o4-mini announcement. No changes made.
